source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/10_자사 품질 매뉴얼, 절차서/영문본/☆ 품질 매뉴얼 및 ISO 절차서 (영문본)/원본/ISO Procedures EN/AQP706 Clinical Evaluation Regulation/AQP706_Clinical Evaluation Regulation.docx

sourceSummary.oneLine: Controlled company regulation defining clinical evaluation planning, data identification, data evaluation, data analysis, reporting, and PMCF expectations for medical devices.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Used to specify the level and justification of clinical evidence required to demonstrate compliance with General Safety and Performance Requirements and to control clinical evaluation plans and reports.
keyPoints: Clinical evaluations must be planned, conducted, documented, and updated throughout the device life cycle.
The required level of clinical evidence must be specified and justified based on device characteristics and intended purpose.
Clinical evaluation follows Step 0 through Step 4: scope and plan, clinical data identification, clinical data evaluation, clinical data analysis, and clinical evaluation report writing.
Clinical evaluation reports must document the evaluation and results, include supporting cross-references, and have date and revision control.
PMCF is treated as a continuous process for updating clinical evaluation under MDR Article 61 and Annex XIV Part A.

entities.name: Quality Management Team
entities.evidence: Written Jeong Gyeong gyun Quality Management Team 2024.04.01.; Approved Hur Nam kyu Quality Management Team 2024.04.01.

entities.name: Quality Manager
entities.evidence: Quality Manager Approval of clinical evaluation plan/report

entities.name: Development Team
entities.evidence: Development Team 1) Oversee clinical evaluation activities 2) Collect clinical data 3) Prepare clinical evaluation plan/report 4) Prepare PMCF plan/report

entities.name: Clinical Evaluation Officer
entities.evidence: Clinical Evaluation Officer Perform clinical evaluation activities

entities.name: MDR (EU) 2017/745
entities.evidence: The development team must plan, conduct and document clinical evaluations in accordance with Article 61 and Annex XIV Part A of MDR (EU) 2017/745

entities.name: MEDDEV 2.7/1 Rev.4
entities.evidence: The clinical evaluation report applies Annex 9 to 11 of MEDDEV 2.7/1 Rev.4.

concepts.name: Clinical Evaluation
concepts.evidence: A systematic and planned process for continuously generating, collecting, analyzing, and evaluating clinical data on a medical device for the purpose of verifying the safety and performance, including clinical benefit, of the device when used as intended by the manufacturer

concepts.name: Clinical Evidence
concepts.evidence: Means the results of clinical evaluations and clinical data of sufficient quantity and quality for a device to enable a qualitative assessment of whether the device is safe and can achieve the intended clinical benefit when used as intended by the manufacturer.

concepts.name: Clinical Data
concepts.evidence: Information on the use of medical devices and their safety or performance from the following sources

concepts.name: General Safety and Performance Requirements
concepts.evidence: This regulation aims to specify the level and justification of clinical evidence required to demonstrate compliance with the General Safety and Performance Requirements (GSPR)

concepts.name: Post-Market Clinical Follow-up
concepts.evidence: PMCF should be understood as a continuous process of updating the clinical evaluation referred to in Article 61 and Annex XIV Part A of MDR (EU) 2017/745

concepts.name: Equivalent device
concepts.evidence: A device proven to be equivalent to the device in question

concepts.name: Clinical Evaluation Report
concepts.evidence: A clinical evaluation report should be prepared to document the clinical evaluation and its results.

evidence.claim: Revision 0 was dated 2024.04.01. and was a complete revision aligned with KGMP, EN ISO 13485:2016+A11:2021, FDA 21 CFR Part 820, and MDR Article 10 requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: 0 2024.04.01. Complete revision in accordance with KGMP, EN ISO 13485:2016+A11:2021, FDA 21 CFR Part 820, and MDR (EU) 2017/745 Article 10 requirements.

evidence.claim: The purpose is to specify the level and justification of clinical evidence required to demonstrate compliance with GSPR for company-manufactured medical devices.
evidence.evidenceText: This regulation aims to specify the level and justification of clinical evidence required to demonstrate compliance with the General Safety and Performance Requirements (GSPR) of medical devices manufactured by our company.

evidence.claim: The procedure applies to planning, continuously conducting, and documenting clinical evaluations of medical devices.
evidence.evidenceText: It is applied to planning, continuously conducting, and documenting clinical evaluations of medical devices.

evidence.claim: Clinical evaluations are required to follow MDR Article 61 and Annex XIV Part A.
evidence.evidenceText: The development team must plan, conduct and document clinical evaluations in accordance with Article 61 and Annex XIV Part A of MDR (EU) 2017/745

evidence.claim: The level of clinical evidence must be specified, justified, and appropriate to device characteristics and intended purpose.
evidence.evidenceText: The level of clinical evidence required to demonstrate compliance with the relevant General Safety and Performance Requirements (GSPR) must be specified and justified. The level of clinical evidence must be appropriate considering the characteristics of the device and its intended purpose.

evidence.claim: Clinical evaluation documentation must be updated throughout the device life cycle using PMCF and PMS data.
evidence.evidenceText: The clinical evaluation and its documentation should be updated throughout the life cycle of the device with clinical data obtained from the implementation of the PMCF plan in accordance with Annex XIV Part B and the post-marketing surveillance (PMS) plan referred to in Article 84 of MDR (EU) 2017/745.

evidence.claim: The clinical evaluation report is part of the technical documentation related to the device.
evidence.evidenceText: The clinical evaluation and its results and the clinical evidence derived therefrom shall be documented in a clinical evaluation report referred to in Section 4 of Annex XIV, which is part of the technical documentation related to the device.

evidence.claim: Clinical evaluation proceeds through Step 0 scope and plan, Step 1 clinical data identification, Step 2 clinical data evaluation, Step 3 clinical data analysis, and Step 4 clinical evaluation report writing.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical evaluation proceeds to the following steps. Step 0: Defining the scope of clinical evaluation and establishing a plan... Step 1: Identification of clinical data... Step 2: Evaluation of clinical data... Step 3: Clinical data analysis... Step 4: Writing a clinical evaluation report

evidence.claim: Equivalence claims require consideration of technical, biological, and clinical characteristics.
evidence.evidenceText: To demonstrate equivalence, the following technical, biological, and clinical characteristics should be considered.

evidence.claim: The clinical evaluation report should include sufficient detail on search criteria, relevant data, assumptions, and conclusions.
evidence.evidenceText: The clinical evaluation report should provide sufficient detail to enable the evaluator to understand the search criteria adopted, the relevant data, any assumptions made, and any conclusions drawn.

evidence.claim: Clinical evaluation reports must have date and revision control.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical evaluation reports must have a date and revision control.

evidence.claim: Clinical Evaluation Plan and Clinical Evaluation Report records are managed by the Development Team with a 10-year storage period.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical Evaluation Plan 706-1 Development Team 10 years; Clinical Evaluation Report 706-2 Development Team 10 years

reviewFlags.reason: No specific product model, size, or device family is identified in the source text.
Authority signal is FDA, but the source also explicitly references KGMP and MDR (EU) 2017/745 requirements.
Some text appears truncated or OCR-degraded, including 'This document is effective from the date of appro.' and 'in accordance with Article 61 and Annex XIV Part A of MDR (EU) 2017/745 to.'
